description: ROX.ONE Agent Workbench Suite: Electron desktop app, server-core, shared agent runtime, MCP sources, skills/workspaces, release validation, security, and testing infrastructure.
repo_notes: This repository uses task tickets in docs/tickets and worklogs in docs/worklog as the development control plane.
DeepWiki is used for broad orientation and generated documentation, but source files and validation commands remain authoritative.
Graphify artifacts are stored locally under /home/dev/craft/rox-one-graphify-deepwiki-2026-05-20/source and should be used for structural code navigation before broad grep.
When answering implementation questions, cite current source files and tests rather than only generated wiki pages.

pages.title: ROX.ONE Overview
pages.prompt: Explain the product, monorepo layout, app/package boundaries, agent runtime, and main user workflows.

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.prompt: Explain how Electron UI, server-core, shared agent runtime, sessions, MCP sources, skills, storage, and RPC/IPC connect.

pages.title: Electron Desktop Application
pages.prompt: Document main process startup, window management, renderer shell, chat UI, workbench screens, settings/account UI, and auto-update.

pages.title: Server and Backend Services
pages.prompt: Document server-core, account/auth, RBAC/security, audit, provider gateway, automations, messaging, and workspace sync.

pages.title: Skills, Workspaces, and Bundles
pages.prompt: Document workspace bundles, skill marketplace, built-in skills, session tools core, transform data, and source integrations.

pages.title: CI/CD and Release Pipeline
pages.prompt: Document GitHub Actions, artifact validation, SBOM, release feed, distribution, E2E smoke, and RC validation.

pages.title: Security and Credentials
pages.prompt: Document credential storage, tenant key derivation, RBAC, audit, supply-chain validation, workflow pins, and security tests.

pages.title: Testing Infrastructure
pages.prompt: Document unit/component tests, audit/static probes, validation scripts, agent contract, and how agents choose the right checks.

pages.title: Agent Development Workflow
pages.prompt: Explain how coding agents should use tickets, worklogs, Graphify, DeepWiki, source inspection, and validation commands.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.prompt: Define codebase-specific concepts such as agent backend, workbench, mission, skill, source, MCP, RBAC, audit, and release gates.
